title: betula alba var. minor
intro: Betula alba var. minor, also known as the dwarf white birch, is a variety of the white birch tree that is native to Europe and Asia. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic compounds, making it useful in treating wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of Betula alba var. minor include reducing fever, relieving rheumatic pain, and acting as an antiseptic to prevent infections. Herbal preparations of this plant include teas, tinctures, and salves, which can be used to treat a variety of ailments, from skin irritations to respiratory issues.
